category_title: Students with non-compliant behavior and difficulty accepting instructions
minimal_context: The student is assigned a spelling task to write definitions for 20 words in an academic setting.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for spelling. You need to write definitions for 20 words today.

STUDENT: (Makes a loud buzzing noise) I am not doing that.

TUTOR: Please get your pencil and start writing.

STUDENT: Shut up, I don't have to listen to your instructions.

TUTOR: You need to finish your spelling work now.

STUDENT: (Rips the spelling worksheet into pieces and throws it) I said no!

options.A: Remain available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near the student and then speaking quietly and gently touching his shoulder.
options.B: Provide the student with a great deal of one-to-one instruction initially, but diminish this support over time.
options.C: Conduct careful pre-intervention functional assessments to identify relationships between the environment and the student's behavior.
options.D: Emphasize mastery goals by valuing personal improvement and mastery of the spelling tasks rather than grades or social comparison.
